content: If we extend the Merriam-Webster definition of an agent as "any entity that exerts power to produce an effect, we can reframe biological/chemical pathogenic agents as naturally occurring 'autonomous agents' that use an implicit ICM-like curiosity mechanism to drive their evolution: they gain 'intrinsic reward' from exploring novel host environments and mutating to exploit unoccupied ecological niches. This cross-domain reframing creates a new framework for predicting pathogen outbreak risk.
